quiz.title: Unit 8 Quiz 8, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Advanced Mole Concepts: Molecules Removed, Mass from Molecules, and Percent Solutions
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molecules to Mass, Mass to Molecules, Percent by Weight Solutions, Removal of Molecules Calculations

questions.question: If $5 \times 10^{22}$ molecules are removed from 500 mg of $\text{H}_2\text{O}$, what is the number of moles of $\text{H}_2\text{O}$ left? (Note: The given data may contain an error; solve based on the intended pattern.)
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Mass = 500 mg = 0.500 g
Molar mass H₂O = 18.02 g/mol
Initial moles = 0.500 / 18.02 = 0.02775 mol
Initial molecules = 0.02775 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $1.67 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Adjust for removal

If $5 \times 10^{21}$ molecules removed (as intended):
Molecules remaining = $1.67 \times 10^{22} - 0.5 \times 10^{22} = 1.17 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $1.17 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0194$ mol

questions.question: The weight of $2 \times 10^{23}$ molecules of $\text{NaCl}$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $2 \times 10^{23} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.332$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass NaCl = 58.44 g/mol
Mass = 0.332 × 58.44 = 19.4 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{NaOH}$ must be dissolved in 100 g of water to prepare a 15% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of NaOH
Solution mass = 100 + x
15% = x / (100 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.15(100 + x) = x
15 + 0.15x = x
15 = 0.85x
x = 15 / 0.85 = 17.65 g ≈ 17.6 g

questions.question: If $10^{20}$ molecules are removed from 1 g of $\text{CO}$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass CO = 28.01 g/mol
Initial moles = 1 / 28.01 = 0.0357 mol
Initial molecules = 0.0357 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $2.15 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $2.15 \times 10^{22} - 0.01 \times 10^{22} = 2.14 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $2.14 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0355$ mol

questions.question: The mass of $3 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of $\text{NH}_3$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $3 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0498$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass NH₃ = 17.03 g/mol
Mass = 0.0498 × 17.03 = 0.848 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{CaCl}_2$ must be added to 80 g of water to make a 25% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of CaCl₂
Solution mass = 80 + x
25% = x / (80 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.25(80 + x) = x
20 + 0.25x = x
20 = 0.75x
x = 20 / 0.75 = 26.67 g ≈ 26.7 g

questions.question: If $4 \times 10^{21}$ molecules are removed from 1.2 g of $\text{O}_2$, what is the number of moles remaining?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass O₂ = 32.00 g/mol
Initial moles = 1.2 / 32.00 = 0.0375 mol
Initial molecules = 0.0375 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $2.258 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $2.258 \times 10^{22} - 0.4 \times 10^{22} = 1.858 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $1.858 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0309$ mol

questions.question: The weight of $5 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of glucose ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_{12}\text{O}_6$) is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $5 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0830$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass glucose = 180.16 g/mol
Mass = 0.0830 × 180.16 = 14.95 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{KBr}$ must be dissolved in 50 g of water to prepare a 10% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of KBr
Solution mass = 50 + x
10% = x / (50 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.10(50 + x) = x
5 + 0.10x = x
5 = 0.90x
x = 5 / 0.90 = 5.56 g

questions.question: If $2 \times 10^{22}$ molecules are removed from 2 g of $\text{HCl}$, how many moles of $\text{HCl}$ are left?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass HCl = 36.46 g/mol
Initial moles = 2 / 36.46 = 0.0549 mol
Initial molecules = 0.0549 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $3.30 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $3.30 \times 10^{22} - 2 \times 10^{22} = 1.30 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $1.30 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0216$ mol

questions.question: The mass of $1 \times 10^{21}$ molecules of $\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3$ is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $1 \times 10^{21} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.00166$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass Fe₂O₃ = 159.70 g/mol
Mass = 0.00166 × 159.70 = 0.265 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{MgSO}_4$ must be dissolved in 120 g of water to make a 12% by weight solution?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of MgSO₄
Solution mass = 120 + x
12% = x / (120 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.12(120 + x) = x
14.4 + 0.12x = x
14.4 = 0.88x
x = 14.4 / 0.88 = 16.36 g ≈ 16.4 g

questions.question: If $8 \times 10^{21}$ molecules are removed from 3 g of $\text{CO}_2$, how many moles remain?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate initial moles and molecules

Molar mass CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Initial moles = 3 / 44.01 = 0.0682 mol
Initial molecules = 0.0682 × $6.022 \times 10^{23}$ = $4.11 \times 10^{22}$ molecules

Step 2: Subtract removed molecules

Molecules remaining = $4.11 \times 10^{22} - 0.8 \times 10^{22} = 3.31 \times 10^{22}$ molecules
Moles remaining = $3.31 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.0550$ mol

questions.question: The weight of $7 \times 10^{22}$ molecules of ethanol ($\text{C}_2\text{H}_5\text{OH}$) is:
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Calculate moles

Moles = $7 \times 10^{22} / 6.022 \times 10^{23} = 0.1163$ mol

Step 2: Calculate mass

Molar mass ethanol = 46.07 g/mol
Mass = 0.1163 × 46.07 = 5.36 g

questions.question: How many grams of $\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3$ are required to make a 20% by weight solution in 200 g of water?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Set up equation

Let x = mass of Na₂CO₃
Solution mass = 200 + x
20% = x / (200 + x) × 100

Step 2: Solve

0.20(200 + x) = x
40 + 0.20x = x
40 = 0.80x
x = 40 / 0.80 = 50.0 g

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered advanced mole concept applications! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review percent solution calculations and molecule removal problems.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the relationship between moles, molecules, and mass.
scoreMessages.0: Please review Avogadro's number, molar mass calculations, and percent by weight formulas.
